title: DSDS Events Document Block
description: Documents the events emitted by a component — what triggers each event, its payload shape, DOM behavior (bubbling, cancelability), and lifecycle information. Separating events from the API block allows tools to render, search, and cross-reference event documentation independently.

eventEntry.description: A single event emitted by a component. Documents the event name, what triggers it, the payload shape consumers receive, DOM propagation behavior, and lifecycle metadata.

name.description: The event name as used in code (e.g., 'onClick', 'onChange', 'onDismiss', 'ds-select'). For custom events, use the full event name including any namespace prefix.

description.description: When this event fires, what it communicates, and how consumers should respond. Include conditions under which the event does NOT fire (e.g., 'Does not fire when disabled').

payload.description: Human-readable description of the event payload shape (e.g., '(event: MouseEvent) => void', '{ value: string, index: number }', 'CustomEvent<{ selectedId: string }>').

payloadSchema.description: Machine-readable JSON Schema (Draft 2020-12) describing the event payload or detail object. Enables tooling to generate type definitions and validate event handlers.

bubbles.description: Whether the event bubbles up through the DOM tree. Defaults to true. Set to false for events that only fire on the target element.

cancelable.description: Whether the event can be cancelled via preventDefault(). Defaults to false. When true, document what cancelling the event prevents.

composed.description: Whether the event crosses shadow DOM boundaries. Defaults to false. Relevant for web components using shadow DOM encapsulation.

since.description: The version in which this event was introduced.

deprecated.description: Whether this event is deprecated. Defaults to false.

deprecationNotice.description: What to use instead when this event is deprecated. Required context when deprecated is true.

examples.description: Usage examples showing how to listen for and handle this event.

events.description: Documents the events emitted by a component as a dedicated document block. Each item describes a single event — its name, trigger conditions, payload shape, DOM behavior, and lifecycle metadata. Separating events from the API block gives tooling a clear, searchable unit for event documentation and allows cross-referencing between events and the states or interactions that trigger them.

kind.description: Identifies this document block as an events spec.

items.description: The events emitted by this component. Each item documents a single event with its name, description, payload, DOM behavior, and lifecycle metadata.

agents.description: Agent-optimized context for this document block. Provides structured information for AI/LLM consumption — constraints, disambiguation, anti-patterns, examples, and keywords.
